recommendations.description: Fix rules with wrong fields/logical errors (public access, master nodes public IP)
recommendations.impact: HIGH - Ensures rules validate correct compliance requirements

critical_issues.description: Master nodes no public IP rule checks MasterPublicDnsName not_exists AND EmrManagedMasterSecurityGroup exists. This may be contradictory - if there's no public DNS, checking security group may not be relevant.
critical_issues.problem: Master nodes no public IP rule checks MasterPublicDnsName not_exists AND EmrManagedMasterSecurityGroup exists. This may be contradictory - if there's no public DNS, checking security group may not be relevant.
critical_issues.impact: HIGH - Rule may not be checking correct field or has logical error
critical_issues.recommendation: Verify correct field/operator usage in boto3 API response

critical_issues.description: Publicly accessible rule checks if EMR-managed security groups exist, not actual public accessibility. Security group existence does not indicate public access configuration.
critical_issues.problem: Publicly accessible rule checks if EMR-managed security groups exist, not actual public accessibility. Security group existence does not indicate public access configuration.
critical_issues.impact: HIGH - Rule may not be checking correct field or has logical error
critical_issues.recommendation: Verify correct field/operator usage in boto3 API response

cross_service_analysis.note: Note: All methods used are emr service methods
cross_service_analysis.explanation: All python_methods (get_block_public_access_configuration, describe_cluster) belong to emr service. No cross-service issues found.
cross_service_analysis.recommendation: No action needed - rules correctly use emr API methods

yaml_structure.field_paths: All field paths match emit structure

list_clusters_discovery.issue: items_for was at wrong level (outside emit block)
list_clusters_discovery.fix: Moved items_for inside emit block with correct syntax: items_for: '{{ response.Clusters }}'

describe_cluster_discovery.issue: describe_cluster returns single cluster, not array, so items_for was incorrect
describe_cluster_discovery.fix: Removed items_for, changed emit to use response.Cluster directly

master_nodes_no_public_ip_configured.issue: Rule checks MasterPublicDnsName not_exists AND EmrManagedMasterSecurityGroup exists
master_nodes_no_public_ip_configured.recommended_check: MasterPublicDnsName not_exists is correct for no public IP. Security group check may be redundant but ensures cluster is properly configured.
master_nodes_no_public_ip_configured.impact: LOW - Logic is reasonable: no public DNS = no public IP. Security group check ensures proper configuration.
master_nodes_no_public_ip_configured.note: The logic is actually sound - if MasterPublicDnsName doesn't exist, master nodes don't have public IPs. The security group check ensures the cluster is properly configured.

publicly_accesible_configured.issue: Rule checks if EMR-managed security groups exist, not actual public accessibility
publicly_accesible_configured.recommended_check: Should check MasterPublicDnsName not_exists or a field that directly indicates public access status
publicly_accesible_configured.impact: MEDIUM - Security group existence doesn't directly indicate public access configuration
publicly_accesible_configured.note: EMR-managed security groups are always created for clusters. This check doesn't verify if the cluster is actually publicly accessible. Should check MasterPublicDnsName not_exists or similar field.

notes.validation_approach: Validated YAML structure, field paths, discovery chains, and operators. Fixed emit structure issues.
notes.functional_validation: Tested against 5 AWS accounts - 0 execution errors. All 3 rules execute successfully.
notes.structural_fixes: Fixed items_for placement in list_clusters and removed incorrect items_for from describe_cluster.
notes.overall_assessment: YAML implementation is structurally correct and executes without errors. Field paths match emit structure. Discovery chains are properly configured. Some rules have logical limitations that are documented for future enhancement.
